bead.title: cli: add ddx bead queue top move clear
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The spec now defines first-class queue movement commands, but operators still only have the generic `ddx bead update --set queue-rank=...` escape hatch. That forces humans to calculate ranks manually and does not protect priority semantics.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:121-137 defines `ddx bead queue top`, `ddx bead queue move --before/--after`, and `ddx bead queue clear` semantics.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:254-261 lists the CLI surface.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:327-337 defines user-story acceptance for queue movement.
- cli/cmd/bead.go:46-65 registers bead subcommands but has no queue subcommand.
- cli/cmd/bead.go:448-540 supports generic update/set/unset but no command that computes sparse ranks or rejects cross-priority moves.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add `ddx bead queue` with `top <id>`, `move <id> --before <other-id>`, `move <id> --after <other-id>`, and `clear <id>`.
- Implement sparse integer rank calculation: top assigns before the first ranked/effective bead in the same priority; before/after uses midpoint when possible; if no midpoint exists, renormalize only the affected priority bucket.
- Reject before/after moves across different priorities with a clear error; priority changes remain explicit via `ddx bead update --priority`.
- Persist queue-rank as a JSON number in Bead.Extra["queue-rank"]; clear removes the key.
- Register the subcommand in newBeadCommand.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Ready/ReadyExecution sorting in this bead; it depends on ddx-5ca2b0af.
- Do not introduce a server-side picker or global queue table.
- Do not add queue-rank to the core Bead struct or known schema field map.
bead.acceptance:
1. TestBeadQueueTopSetsRankWithoutChangingPriority verifies `ddx bead queue top <id>` persists numeric queue-rank and leaves Priority unchanged.
2. TestBeadQueueMoveBeforeAfterUsesSparseRanks verifies before/after placement produces the expected ReadyExecution order without rewriting unrelated priority buckets.
3. TestBeadQueueMoveRejectsCrossPriority verifies before/after across different priorities returns a clear error and does not mutate either bead.
4. TestBeadQueueClearRemovesQueueRank verifies `ddx bead queue clear <id>` removes the key and default ordering resumes.
5. TestBeadQueueRenormalizesOnlyPriorityBucket verifies midpoint exhaustion renormalizes only beads in the affected priority bucket.
6. WIRED-IN: newBeadCommand registers `newBeadQueueCommand`, and `ddx bead queue --help` lists top, move, and clear.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run "TestBeadQueue" -count=1 passes.
8. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
